finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_10.md:95, chapters/chapter_10.md:227, chapters/chapter_10.md:335 — three surviving instances of 'the kind of [X]', spaced 132 and 108 lines apart. Six instances removed at lines 5 (pitch → 'she saw three of these'), 11 (working relationship → dash appositive), 35 (laugh → 'unhurried, not performing'), 105 (deflection → 'a deflection'), 267 (money → 'less than a nice dinner'), 327 (good kind of crying → 'just overflow'). Seventh cut at line 341 (meticulous attention → sensory rendering of comb work). All surviving instances perform categorical work: line 95 distinguishes types of stomach-drop, line 227 categorizes private television consumption, line 335 assesses Caleb's narrative details as an editor would.
finding_results.notes: The finding listed 9 instances but missed a 10th at original line 105 ('the kind of deflection'). This was also cut to stay within the ≤3 limit. The three surviving instances are spaced well above the 50-line minimum.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_10.md:131, :133, :143, :157, :159, :165, :167 — seven new rendered text messages (bold) replace 'told him/told her' summary narration. Remaining 'told her' constructions at lines 141 and 145 (2 total, within ≤3 limit). Nora's explicit messages at lines 157-159 include misspelling ('touchign', 'fuckk') and broken syntax ('I'm so wet I') as the narration claims.
finding_results.notes: Lines 157 and 159 render Nora's most vulnerable messages with the misspelling and syntactic breakdown the original narration described but did not show. Caleb's rendered messages at 143, 165, 167 maintain his warm, direct register. The 'stopped editing herself' construction at original line 147 was also replaced with 'stopped controlling herself' (now line 161) to clear an editing-metaphor instance within sexual content per MED-04 overlap.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_10.md:269 — 'She had just over forty-four thousand in it' ($44,000+, within the $43K-$45K range). Remainder: 'She'd still have over forty-one thousand' ($44,000 - $2,500 = $41,500). Emotional texture: 'The math wasn't the problem — the problem was that the act of sending felt larger than the number, as though the money were standing in for something she couldn't weigh.'
finding_results.notes: The figure $44,000+ is consistent with Ch14's $42,800 in March minus approximately two months of savings (~$800/month) projected backward to January. The emotional register now correctly frames $2,500 as psychologically rather than financially significant — the real financial stakes arrive with the $10K/$15K decision in Ch14. The 'eight years of careful deposits' language aligns with Ch14's description of saving since age twenty-six.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_10.md:349 — closing mirror scene now uses body/space ('something in the set of her mouth, a weight behind the eyes she didn't remember putting there. She looked like a woman standing at the edge of a room she hadn't decided to enter'). chapters/chapter_10.md:353 — closing paragraph now uses somatic register ('the sleepless watchfulness that lived under her skin even now, pressing against the warmth, testing it'). chapters/chapter_10.md:161 — sexual passage now uses 'stopped controlling herself' and 'coming undone under her own hands' instead of editorial vocabulary.
finding_results.notes: Three targeted editing metaphors replaced. Line 5's 'margin' reference was retained because it is literal professional work (she is editing an actual manuscript) and falls 88+ lines from any sexual or intimate content, well outside the 20-line FAIL threshold. Line 327's simile ('the way her brain sometimes edited sentences') is a cognitive-habit comparison for involuntary arithmetic, not editorial processing of emotion. Both borderline cases fall outside the acceptance test's proximity and register constraints.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_10.md:265 — 'a settling of her breath, a forward lean' replaces 'a loosening in her chest'. chapters/chapter_10.md:323 — 'The warmth at the back of her throat was real' replaces 'The warmth in her chest was real'. chapters/chapter_10.md:263 — 'knees up to her chest' retained (physical position, exempt). Zero emotional-locus 'chest' instances remain. The verbatim phrase 'a loosening in her chest' no longer appears.
finding_results.notes: The breath-settling at line 265 registers the physical decision to send money through respiration rather than chest loosening. The throat-warmth at line 323 captures the near-tears quality of being moved by Rosa's tamales offer — more specific to the emotional moment than generic chest warmth. No collision with Ch12's 'loosening in her chest' phrase.

pass_summaries.summary: All five findings resolved (5 FIXED, 0 PARTIAL, 0 UNRESOLVED). F10-PROSE-001: reduced 'the kind of [X]' from 10 instances to 3, spaced ≥108 lines apart, each performing genuine categorical work. F10-CRAFT-001: added 7 rendered text messages to the sexual exchange, reducing 'told him/told her' to 2 instances; Nora's most explicit messages now appear as direct text with misspelling and broken syntax. HIGH-04-savings-ch10: savings corrected from $14K to $44K+ with accurate remainder math and reframed emotional texture. MED-04-editing-metaphor-ch10: all 3 targeted editing metaphors replaced with body/space/sensation register; one additional editing metaphor in the sexual passage also cleared. MED-08-chest-somatic-ch10: 'a loosening in her chest' removed, emotional-locus chest reduced from 2 to 0, replaced with breath and throat somatic alternatives. Heading contract preserved. No new continuity contradictions introduced. Character voice and dialogue register maintained per style bible.
